data.title: An InventHelp 123Invent Client Develops New Accessory for Fastening Zippers (KSG-172)
data.description:
An InventHelp 123Invent Client Develops New Accessory for Fastening Zippers (KSG-172)

An InventHelp 123Invent Client Develops New Accessory for Fastening Zippers (KSG-172)

PR Newswire

PITTSBURGH, April 1, 2026

PITTSBURGH, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- "I wanted to create a decorative accessory that would ease the task a fastening a zipper on a bag or clothing item," said an inventor from Sturgis, Michigan. As a result, they developed ZIPPER HELPERS, designed to provide an enhanced grip, and to prevent the pull tab from getting stuck at the base.

InventHelp Invention Spotlight: ZIPPER HELPERS Solves Everyday Zipper Challenges

  • Provides an accessory to assist in fastening a zipper.
  • Can be used with the zipper on a purse, bag, tote, jacket, etc.
  • Eliminates the need to struggle with the traditional pull tab.

InventHelp Client's Invention for the General Population & Those with Limited Manual Dexterity
With more consumers seeking an easier and more stylish way to fastener zippers, there may be a demand for new zipper accessories. ZIPPER HELPERS could fill this niche by offering a functional and fashionable design that is easy to apply and use.

Potential Licensing Opportunities for InventHelp 123Invent Client:

  • The design has been submitted to the National sales office of InventHelp.
  • The invention is available for licensing or sale to manufacturers or marketers who may wish to bring it to market.
  • For more information, visit www.zipperhelpers.com or email info@zipperhelpers.com.

About InventHelp: Empowering Inventors with Assistance, Guidance, and Potential Visibility
InventHelp is one of America's largest and longest-running invention-service companies, helping inventors and entrepreneurs. Since 1984, the company has assisted clients with invention submission, prototype modeling services, invention websites, patent referral services, and more. InventHelp's provides services on how to present, promote and protect inventions. InventHelp has a network of regional sales offices across the U.S. and Canada. With its Invention Services, InventHelp provides confidentiality, step-by-step support, and help to innovators who wish to submit their inventions to industry, either through us or on their own. To learn more about InventHelp, visit InventHelp.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/an-inventhelp-123invent-client-develops-new-accessory-for-fastening-zippers-ksg-172-302731665.html

SOURCE InventHelp

data.title: Cliff Lede Vineyards Appoints Trellis Wine & Spirits as Exclusive On-Premise National Accounts Partner
data.description:
Cliff Lede Vineyards Appoints Trellis Wine & Spirits as Exclusive On-Premise National Accounts Partner

Cliff Lede Vineyards Appoints Trellis Wine & Spirits as Exclusive On-Premise National Accounts Partner

PR Newswire

YOUNTVILLE, Calif., April 1, 2026

YOUNTVILLE, Calif., April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Cliff Lede Vineyards, a multi-generational family-owned and operated winery based in Napa Valley's Stags Leap District, is pleased to announce the addition of Trellis Wine & Spirits to its sales and marketing team.

"We are excited to launch this new partnership with a like-minded family operation," says General Manager and COO Jason Lede. "Trellis has a proven track record of helping small wineries like ours gain a foothold and grow in an important sales channel. We are optimistic this partnership will lead to our shared vision of success."

"Cliff Lede is a highly respected name in Napa and a producer with family pedigree, an irreverent point of view, and fantastic wines," says Mark Crisler, Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Trellis Wine & Spirits. "We look forward to welcoming them into our portfolio of brands and achieving success with our new partnership."

Since 2002, Cliff Lede Vineyards has distributed the highlights of its portfolio to top on-premise and retail accounts throughout California, the US, and select international markets. Wines in distribution include Sauvignon Blanc, Napa Valley; Cabernet Sauvignon, Napa Valley; Chardonnay, Carneros, Napa Valley; "High Fidelity" Red Wine, Napa Valley; "Rhythm" Cabernet Sauvignon, Stags Leap District; and the multi-100-point "Poetry" Cabernet Sauvignon, Stags Leap District.

Effective immediately, Trellis Wine & Spirits will lead national on-premise chain sales for the portfolio.

For more information on Cliff Lede wines in distribution, please visit:

https://www.cliffledevineyards.com/trade

About Cliff Lede Vineyards

Established by the Lede family in 2002 with the purchase of 60 acres in the Stags Leap District, Cliff Lede Vineyards has become a leading family-owned and operated winery with more than 160 acres of estate vineyards across Napa Valley. The highlights of their vineyard portfolio include the steep rocky hillside Poetry Vineyard and the neighboring Rhythm Vineyard on their Stags Leap District estate, in addition to their Calistoga and Carneros properties. Over the last two decades, the winery's flagship estate bottling, Poetry Cabernet Sauvignon, has been awarded multiple 100-point scores across critical publications. Founded by Cliff Lede, General Manager & COO Jason Lede oversees operations, while winemaking is led by Director of Winemaking Christopher Tynan and Winemaker Travis Bullard. The Cliff Lede Vineyards tasting room offers a complete Napa Valley lifestyle experience where the best in wine, music, art, architecture, and nature intersect. A variety of unique tastings and private tours, including the Backstage Tasting Lounge experience, are available daily from 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.

About Trellis Wine & Spirits

Founded in 2005 by Mark Crisler, Trellis Wine & Spirits represents a curated portfolio of independent, terroir-driven wine, spirits, and beverage producers, connecting them with premier restaurant, hotel, and hospitality groups nationwide. Drawing on decades of industry experience, Crisler established Trellis to expand the range and sophistication of national beverage programs.

Today, Trellis is a respected, top-tier partner with extensive national placements, specializing in premium and luxury brands. The company provides strategic access to key decision-makers while supporting the growing demand for distinctive, provenance-driven wines and beverages.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/cliff-lede-vineyards-appoints-trellis-wine--spirits-as-exclusive-on-premise-national-accounts-partner-302730571.html

SOURCE Cliff Lede Vineyards

data.title: Jarrett Opens 408,000-Square-Foot Warehouse Facility in Wooster, Ohio
data.description:
Jarrett Opens 408,000-Square-Foot Warehouse Facility in Wooster, Ohio

Jarrett Opens 408,000-Square-Foot Warehouse Facility in Wooster, Ohio

PR Newswire

ORRVILLE, Ohio, April 1, 2026

Surpassing One Million Square Feet of Warehouse Space Nationwide

ORRVILLE, Ohio, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Jarrett, an award-winning third-party logistics provider, announced the official opening of its newest warehouse facility, encompassing 408,000 square feet, located at Wooster's Innovation Park. The opening marks a milestone for the company, pushing its total warehouse footprint over one million square feet and further strengthening its national distribution network.

"Reaching over a million square feet is a direct result of the partnerships we've built with our customers over the past 26 years. The Wooster facility gives us the capacity and technology to continue delivering the level of service our clients expect as their supply chains grow," said Mike Jarrett, Founder, President & CEO of Jarrett.

The newly constructed Wooster facility features a cloud-based Warehouse Management System, jPICK, which offers real-time shipment visibility tools and full API and EDI integration, through the company's Jarrett LINC technology, enabling seamless connectivity with customers' existing platforms. The facility is also expected to obtain food-grade certification, broadening the range of industries Jarrett can serve at this location.

"The Wooster facility was designed from the ground up with our customers' needs in mind. Pairing our world-class technology with our hard-working employees allows us to deliver the real-time visibility and seamless execution that today's shippers demand, along with the flexibility to scale alongside them," said Aaron Nussbaum, VP of Logistics Operations at Jarrett.

The Wooster warehouse will have rooftop solar panels installed later this year, which will make it the largest fully solar-powered building in Wayne County. A rooftop solar array, motion-sensor LED lighting, high R-value insulation and a fleet of electric forklifts give the facility an industry-leading sustainability profile. For supply chain customers with ESG commitments, the facility provides a verifiable, low-carbon warehousing option backed by clean energy infrastructure.

With this newest Wooster location opening, Jarrett now operates over one million square feet of warehouse space across Ohio, Pennsylvania and Utah. The new facility extends the company's Midwest presence and works in collaboration with Jarrett's nearby Orrville headquarters and 24/7/365 Jarrett Routing Center, providing continuous shipment visibility and execution support for customers from the eastern seaboard to the west coast.

To learn more about Jarrett's warehousing and logistics solutions, visit www.gojarrett.com. 

About Jarrett
Jarrett is an award-winning third-party logistics provider delivering comprehensive logistics, warehousing, international and fleet solutions. Founded in 1999, the family-owned company is headquartered in Orrville, Ohio, where its 24-hour Jarrett Routing Center provides around-the-clock shipment visibility and execution. With strategically located warehouse facilities in Ohio, Pennsylvania and Utah, Jarrett supports customers nationwide with scalable, technology-driven supply chain solutions. Recognized as a market leader in the industry, Jarrett partners with many of the world's leading brands to optimize performance, increase visibility and drive measurable results.

MEDIA CONTACT: Jacquie Mazziotta, Senior Marketing Manager, jmazziotta@gojarrett.com / (330) 682.0099, ext. 2024

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/jarrett-opens-408-000-square-foot-warehouse-facility-in-wooster-ohio-302731626.html

SOURCE Jarrett

data.title: 'America's Best in Medicine' Honoree Amy Dunn Launches Wildwood Pelvic Health, Filling a Vital Gap in Idaho's Healthcare Crisis
data.description:
'America's Best in Medicine' Honoree Amy Dunn Launches Wildwood Pelvic Health, Filling a Vital Gap in Idaho's Healthcare Crisis

'America's Best in Medicine' Honoree Amy Dunn Launches Wildwood Pelvic Health, Filling a Vital Gap in Idaho's Healthcare Crisis

PR Newswire

BOISE, Idaho, April 1, 2026

BOISE, Idaho, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Amy Dunn, NP, a distinguished medical provider recently recognized as an "America's Best in Medicine" honoree by the American Health Council, proudly announces the grand opening of Wildwood Pelvic Health, a specialized urology and gynecology clinic in Boise. The clinic launches at a critical time as Idaho faces a growing shortage of healthcare providers—particularly those offering advanced care for urologic, hormonal, and sexual health.

Wildwood Pelvic Health provides a modern, evidence-based practice focused on adult wellness. The clinic addresses complex and often overlooked conditions affecting urinary health, sexual wellness, hormonal balance, and quality of life. Through highly personalized care and advanced medical management, Dunn is helping bridge a significant gap for patients who struggle to find specialized attention within larger healthcare systems.

With more than 15 years of clinical experience, Dunn brings a distinctive blend of expertise in urology, gynecology, hormone optimization, and sexual medicine, allowing her to treat interconnected conditions that impact overall health and vitality. Her approach emphasizes comprehensive evaluation, individualized treatment plans, and compassionate care for sensitive health concerns affecting adults at every stage of life.

The clinic offers a comprehensive suite of services designed to support adult health and vitality, including:

  • Women's Health & Gynecology: Comprehensive gynecological care, menopause management, and specialized treatments designed to support women's wellness at every stage of life.
  • Hormone Optimization: Specialized Hormone Replacement Therapy (HRT) for women and Testosterone Replacement Therapy (TRT) for men to restore balance, energy, and long-term wellness.
  • Sexual Health & Performance: Advanced medical treatments for erectile dysfunction (ED), libido concerns, and sexual wellness for both men and women.
  • Urologic Care: Expert evaluation and management of urinary concerns, including recurrent UTIs, urinary urgency, and incontinence.

Operating through both a physical clinic in Boise and a secure telehealth platform, Wildwood Pelvic Health expands access to specialized care across Idaho, ensuring patients can receive expert guidance regardless of geographic barriers.

Wildwood Pelvic Health is a woman-owned specialty clinic located in Boise, Idaho. Founded by Amy Dunn, NP, the practice provides advanced care in urology, gynecology, hormone health, and sexual medicine. The clinic delivers compassionate, judgment-free medical care while helping men and women optimize their health, vitality, and quality of life.

Patients ready to prioritize their health and well-being can learn more or schedule a consultation at https://wildwoodpelvichealth.com/

Contact Information
Name: Amy Dunn
Email: 411295@email4pr.com
Phone Number: (208) 957-3978

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/americas-best-in-medicine-honoree-amy-dunn-launches-wildwood-pelvic-health-filling-a-vital-gap-in-idahos-healthcare-crisis-302731625.html

SOURCE Wildwood Pelvic Health

An InventHelp 123Invent Client Develops New Way to Create an Outdoor Drain (SBT-2342)

PITTSBURGH, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- "I wanted to create a better drain option for residential and commercial locations that would be easy to install," said an inventor from Running Springs, California. As a result, they developed THE K TRAY, designed specifically to offer a versatile and attractive option that looks nicer than expensive drain boxes.

InventHelp Invention Spotlight: THE K TRAY Offers New Outdoor Drain Option

With more consumers looking to save costs when installing outdoor drains, there may be a demand for new alternatives to traditional drain boxes. THE K TRAY would provide a durable and cost-effective design that is easy to install.

data.title: In HelloNation, Home Health Expert Leslie Knopp Highlights Small Home Adjustments for Older Adults
data.description:
In HelloNation, Home Health Expert Leslie Knopp Highlights Small Home Adjustments for Older Adults

In HelloNation, Home Health Expert Leslie Knopp Highlights Small Home Adjustments for Older Adults

PR Newswire

TRAVERSE CITY, Mich., April 1, 2026

TRAVERSE CITY, Mich., April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- How can small changes at home help older adults feel more capable and secure each day? A HelloNation article provides the answer by exploring how thoughtful home adjustments support safety, confidence, and independence. In the article, Home Health Expert Leslie Knopp of Comfort Keepers of Northwest Michigan explains how supportive living spaces can make daily routines easier while helping older adults remain comfortable aging in place.

The article explains that confidence at home is shaped not only by health or mobility, but also by how easily a space supports everyday movement. When items are easy to reach and rooms are simple to navigate, older adults can complete tasks without unnecessary effort. These supportive living spaces reduce frustration and help preserve confidence at home through practical, intentional design.

A central focus of the article is aging in place and the role the environment plays in making that possible. Familiar surroundings offer emotional comfort, but they also support function when arranged thoughtfully. By reducing obstacles and simplifying layout, the home becomes a place that encourages daily independence rather than hesitation.

Senior home safety is highlighted as a key benefit of small adjustments. The article explains that improved lighting, especially in hallways, kitchens, and bathrooms, helps older adults see clearly and move with greater certainty. When visibility improves, hesitation decreases, which supports safer movement and reinforces confidence at home.

Clutter is addressed as both a physical and emotional concern. Clear walkways reduce tripping hazards and support fall prevention, but they also create a calmer environment. The article notes that reducing visual distraction helps older adults focus on what they want to do, supporting daily independence and emotional ease.

Furniture placement is another area where small changes make a difference. Chairs with sturdy arms and clear paths between rooms allow for safer transitions between sitting and standing. These thoughtful choices contribute to senior home safety while encouraging movement that feels natural and unforced.

The article also emphasizes the importance of home modifications for seniors in high use areas like kitchens and bathrooms. Non slip flooring, easy to grip handles, and accessible storage support safer routines. These practical details allow older adults to maintain privacy and dignity while completing essential tasks independently.

Consistency within the home is shown to support both memory and routine. The article explains that keeping commonly used items in familiar places reduces confusion and mental strain. This predictability helps older adults feel more in control, reinforcing confidence at home and supporting aging in place.

Supportive living spaces are also linked to ongoing movement. When fear of falling is reduced, older adults are more likely to stay active within their home. Regular movement supports strength and balance, further contributing to fall prevention and long term daily independence.

The article highlights that home modifications for seniors do not require major renovations. Small, subtle adjustments can blend seamlessly into existing decor. Maintaining familiarity helps preserve emotional connection to the home while still improving safety and function.

Confidence at home also affects social engagement. The article explains that when older adults feel secure moving through their space, they are more comfortable welcoming others. This sense of ease supports emotional well-being and reduces isolation, reinforcing the value of senior home safety beyond physical protection.

The article notes that supportive living spaces should evolve as needs change. Being proactive with small updates helps maintain aging in place without overwhelming disruption. This flexible approach supports long term comfort and daily independence.

Families often notice positive emotional changes when a home becomes easier to navigate. Reduced fear and frustration can lead to calmer moods and stronger relationships. These outcomes highlight how fall prevention and confidence at home work together to support quality of life.

The article concludes by emphasizing that small home adjustments are about empowerment, not limitation. Thoughtful changes support daily independence, protect senior home safety, and strengthen confidence at home. These supportive living spaces help older adults focus on living well while continuing to age in place.

The article, Small Home Adjustments That Make a Big Difference for Older Adults, features insights from Leslie Knopp, Home Health Expert of Traverse City, MI, in HelloNation.

About HelloNation
HelloNation is a premier media platform that connects readers with trusted professionals and businesses across various industries. Through its innovative "edvertising" approach that blends educational content and storytelling, HelloNation delivers expert-driven articles that inform, inspire, and empower. Covering topics from home improvement and health to business strategy and lifestyle, HelloNation highlights leaders making a meaningful impact in their communities.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/in-hellonation-home-health-expert-leslie-knopp-highlights-small-home-adjustments-for-older-adults-302731604.html

SOURCE HelloNation

data.title: Kering Sells Milan Building to Qatar Fund in Latest Portfolio Reshuffle
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby 




  Kering is selling off most of its stake in a prestigious Milan building just two years after acquiring it for a billion and a half dollars. 



  The French luxury-fashion group, owner of Gucci and other high-end brands, said Wednesday that it will sell the asset to a newly formed joint company held 80% by Qatari investment fund Al Mirqab. Kering will retain a stake of 20% in the company. The group will get immediate proceeds of 729 million euros ($842.3 million) on closing, with another 432 million euros to be paid in five years, it said. 



  The deal comes barely two years after Kering agreed to acquire the historic building, situated on a corner of the glamorous Quadrilatero della Moda in a swanky part of Italy's business and fashion capital. The group in 2024 paid around 1.3 billion euros to a subsidiary of Blackstone for the building. It said at the time that the investment was part of a strategy aimed at securing top locations for its fashion houses. 



  The decision to sell the building comes as Kering, under the new leadership of former Renault boss Luca de Meo, looks to move on from a difficult period for the group, which has seen Gucci struggle with falling sales. Last year, the company sold stakes in real-estate assets in Paris and New York, and in October it reached a deal to sell its beauty business to French giant L'Oreal for some $4.7 billion. 





  Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 01, 2026 12:33 ET (16:33 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Neal Gerber Eisenberg Becomes First Law Firm to Achieve GBI Journey to Net Zero Certification for Both Energy and Carbon
data.description:
Neal Gerber Eisenberg Becomes First Law Firm to Achieve GBI Journey to Net Zero Certification for Both Energy and Carbon

Neal Gerber Eisenberg (NGE) is proud to announce that it has achieved Certified Status for both Energy and Carbon through the Green Building Initiative, Inc.’s (GBI) Journey to Net Zero for Sustainable Interiors assessment, following a comprehensive evaluation of the firm’s three-floor office build-out. NGE is the first law firm globally to earn certification in both Energy and Carbon under this program.

GBI’s Journey to Net Zero for Sustainable Interiors program is a rigorous, performance-based assessment designed to support the decarbonization of the built environment. The certification evaluates a project’s energy use intensity (EUI) and operational carbon emissions against advanced baseline standards, confirming that a space has achieved a 100% reduction in energy and carbon impacts through efficiency, electrification strategies, and verified renewable energy sourcing.

Achieving Certified Status signals that NGE’s interior space meets these demanding criteria today, while also providing a framework for continued verification and improvement over time. The certification is valid for three years and is intended to encourage ongoing accountability as organizations progress on their path toward long-term net zero goals.

“This certification reflects years of intentional decisions rather than a single initiative,” said Sonia Menon, Chief Operating Officer of NGE. “From energy modeling and material selection to how we procure power and operate our space, sustainability is embedded into how our office functions every day. We’re proud to be the first law firm to meet GBI’s Energy and Carbon standards and hope it demonstrates what is possible for professional services firms committed to measurable, accountable progress.”

This achievement builds on NGE’s broader sustainability leadership. Last July, NGE earned LEED Platinum Certification for its newly built-out office, becoming the only law firm in Chicago to achieve that distinction and the second-largest LEED Platinum Certified interiors project in Illinois. That certification recognized the firm’s performance across water efficiency, energy optimization, indoor environmental quality and materials transparency.

“Achieving Certified Status for both Energy and Carbon under GBI’s Journey to Net Zero program requires more than good intentions. It requires verified performance,” said Laci Hoskins, Principal at Novo Sustainability. “NGE’s certification reflects a high level of rigor, strong documentation, and a clear commitment to operating their space at a verified net zero energy and carbon level under the program’s standards.”

Beyond the physical workspace, NGE continues to advance sustainability through firm-wide initiatives, community engagement, and transparent reporting. The firm recently released its Corporate Social Responsibility Report, detailing progress across environmental stewardship, pro bono service, community partnerships and internal education efforts led by its Sustainability Committee.

About NGE

Neal Gerber Eisenberg (NGE) is a leading law firm trusted by entrepreneurs, public companies, private businesses, and their owners to handle sophisticated legal matters. For forty years, NGE has built enduring partnerships with clients across the globe, delivering personalized counsel and practical solutions tailored to each unique need. More than one-third of the firm’s attorneys are recognized in the latest Best Lawyers in America listing. NGE represents numerous Fortune 100 companies, as well as many of the nation’s most respected private enterprises. The firm is also a valued advisor to startups and growth-stage companies, offering strategic counsel that evolves with their business.

Alexis A. Johnson
Director of Marketing & Business Development
Neal, Gerber & Eisenberg LLP
p: 312.269.3081
e: aajohnson@nge.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260401679688/en/

data.title: ACE Cash Express Raises $74,192 for Humane World for Animals
data.description:
ACE Cash Express Raises $74,192 for Humane World for Animals

ACE Cash Express Raises $74,192 for Humane World for Animals

PR Newswire

DALLAS, April 1, 2026

DALLAS, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Humane World for Animals (HWFA) is a valued ACE Cash Express (ACE), a Populus Financial Group, Inc. (Populus) brand, charity partner, and an important part of ACE's Chip In For Charity initiative. This year, ACE expanded its relationship with HWFA to be a More Than a Pet campaign sponsor.

The More Than a Pet campaign raises awareness and support for expanding access to affordable veterinary care, pet food, and pet-inclusive housing. In the U.S. alone, more than 20 million companion animals experience poverty with their families and 70% of those animals have never seen a veterinarian In fact, 43% of all pet owners have been unable to pay for their pets' needs at some point due to financial reasons.  

Last year, ACE employees volunteered at the Los Angeles More Than a Pet Unpacking Pawty, distributing 17,308 pounds of pet food, providing 121,800 meals, and supporting over 300 people and pets - all at no cost to families.

ACE donated $74,192 to HWFA through its Flare Account® Chip In For Charity initiative1 and Porte's® #DoorToChange2 charitable giving program. ACE's Chip in for Charity program allows customers to support one of several charities simply by using their co-branded Flare Account® charity debit card wherever they shop. When the card is swiped, ACE donates an amount equal to a portion of every purchase made to the associated charity up to preset limits. Populus added HWFA as a Porte #DoorToChange charity in the Porte app. Porte members simply select HWFA as their #DoorToChange charity, and at no additional cost, Populus makes contributions based on card usage.

"We're thrilled that Populus Financial Group has continued their support - from the Chip in for Charity and Door to Change programs to More Than a Pet - making a difference for pets and families in need," said Kristie Jones, Director of Global Strategic Partnerships at Humane World for Animals.

"We would like to thank our employees and customers for supporting HWFA by using our Flare Account Chip In For Charity and Porte's #DoorToChange charitable giving programs," said Sally Aiello, Vice President of Government Relations at Populus. "This significant contribution truly makes a difference by expanding access to vital veterinary care and providing pet food for families and their beloved animals."

For information about the Flare Account Chip in for Charity program, visit https://www.flareaccount.com/charity-cards/. For information about the Porte #DoortoChange charitable giving program, visit joinporte.com/features/door-to-change.

1ACE Cash Express donates $0.05 for each $100.00 in debit card purchase transactions (net of refunds and chargebacks) made with a Humane World for Animals branded debit card associated with a Flare Account® to Humane World for Animals, a 501(c)(3) organization. Pathward®, N.A., Ouro Global, Inc., and Visa® do not endorse or sponsor this offer.

2After selecting a charity through your mobile app, Populus will donate an amount equal to 0.05% of every debit card purchase transaction, subject to certain terms and conditions described in the mobile app, made with your Porte Debit Card to a charity selected by you through the mobile app. Pathward, N.A., Ouro Global, Inc., and Visa are not affiliated in any way with this offer and do not endorse or sponsor this offer.

The Flare Account® is a demand deposit account established by Pathward, N.A., Member FDIC, pursuant to a license from Visa U.S.A. Inc. Ouro Global, Inc. is a service provider to Pathward N.A..

Card can be used everywhere Visa® debit cards are accepted.

Porte is a deposit account established by Pathward N.A., Member FDIC, pursuant to a license from Visa U.S.A. Inc. Certain products and services may be licensed under U.S. Patent Nos. 6,000,608 and 6,189,787. Card can be used everywhere Visa debit cards are accepted.

About Populus Financial Group

Populus Financial Group® provides financial services through its family of brands including ACE Cash Express®, ACE Elite® Visa® Prepaid Debit Card, Flare Account® and Porte®. Populus Financial Group delivers a broad range of financial products and services including short-term consumer loans, card services, check cashing, money transfers, bill payments and money orders. Visit PopulusFinancial.com for more information.

About the Humane World for Animals

Together, we tackle the root causes of animal cruelty and suffering to create permanent change. With millions of supporters and work happening in over 50 countries, Humane World for Animals—formerly called the Humane Society of the United States and Humane Society International—addresses the most deeply entrenched forms of animal cruelty and suffering. As the leading voice in the animal protection space, we work to end the cruelest practices, care for animals in crisis and build a stronger animal protection movement. Driving toward the greatest global impact, we aim to achieve the vision behind our name: a more humane world.
humaneworld.org

About Pathward®

Pathward®, N.A., a national bank, is a subsidiary of Pathward Financial, Inc. Pathward is focused on financial access and strives to increase financial availability, choice and opportunity across our Partner Solutions and Commercial Finance business lines. The strategic business lines provide support to individuals and businesses. Learn more at Pathward.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/ace-cash-express-raises-74-192-for-humane-world-for-animals-302731484.html

SOURCE Populus Financial Group, Inc.

data.title: Interactive Brokers Group Reports Brokerage Metrics and Other Financial Information for March 2026, includes Reg.-NMS Execution Statistics
data.description:
Interactive Brokers Group Reports Brokerage Metrics and Other Financial Information for March 2026, includes Reg.-NMS Execution Statistics

Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: IBKR) an automated global electronic broker, today reported its Electronic Brokerage monthly performance metrics for March.

Brokerage highlights for the month included:

  • 4.329 million Daily Average Revenue Trades (DARTs)1, 25% higher than prior year and 1% lower than prior month.
  • Ending client equity of $789.4 billion, 38% higher than prior year and 4% lower than prior month.
  • Ending client margin loan balances of $86.0 billion, 35% higher than prior year and 4% lower than prior month.
  • Ending client credit balances of $168.8 billion, including $6.5 billion in insured bank deposit sweeps2, 35% higher than prior year and 4% higher than prior month.
  • 4.754 million client accounts, 31% higher than prior year and 2% higher than prior month.
  • 199 annualized average cleared DARTs1 per client account.
  • Average commission per cleared Commissionable Order3 of $2.74 including exchange, clearing and regulatory fees. Key products:

Futures include options on futures. We estimate exchange, clearing and regulatory fees to be 58% of the futures commissions.

Other financial information for Interactive Brokers Group:

  • Mark to market on U.S. government securities portfolio4 was a loss of $772,000 for the quarter ended March 31st.
  • GLOBAL5: The value of the GLOBAL, reported in U.S. dollars, decreased by 0.54% in March and decreased by 0.57% for the quarter ended March 31st. 

In the interest of transparency, we quantify our IBKR PRO clients’ all-in cost of trade execution below.

For the full multimedia release with graph see link:

https://www.interactivebrokers.com/MonthlyMetrics

  • Average U.S. Reg-NMS stock trade was $24,483 in March (dividing 2c by 1a in table below).
  • In March, IBKR PRO clients’ total cost of executing and clearing U.S. Reg.-NMS stocks through IB was about 1.7 basis points of trade money6, as, measured against a daily VWAP7 benchmark (2.3 basis points net cost for the rolling twelve months).

The above illustrates that the rolling twelve months’ average all-in cost of an IBKR PRO client U.S. Reg.-NMS stock trade was 2.3 basis points.

________________

Note 1: Daily Average Revenue Trades (DARTs) – customer orders divided by the number of trading days in the period.

Note 2: FDIC insured client bank deposit sweep program balances with participating banks. These deposits are not reported in the Company’s statement of financial condition.

Note 3: Commissionable Order – a customer order that generates commissions.

Note 4: Mark to market gains and losses on investments in U.S. government securities and associated hedges are included in Other Income. In the general course of business, we hold these investments to maturity. As a result, accumulated mark to market gains or losses should converge to zero at maturity. Accounting conventions require broker-dealers, unlike banks, to mark all investments to market.

Note 5: In connection with our currency diversification strategy, we have determined to base our net worth in GLOBALs, a basket of 10 major currencies in which we hold our equity. The total effect of the currency diversification strategy is reported in Comprehensive Income and the components are reported in (1) Other Income and (2) Other Comprehensive Income (“OCI”) on the balance sheet. The effect of the GLOBAL on our comprehensive income can be estimated by multiplying the total equity for the period by the change in the U.S. dollar value of the GLOBAL during the same period.

Note 6: Trade money is the total amount of money clients spent or received, including all commissions and fees.

Note 7: Consistent with the clients’ trading activity, the computed VWAP benchmark includes extended trading hours.

________________

More information, including historical results for each of the above metrics, can be found on the investor relations page of the Company’s corporate web site, www.interactivebrokers.com/ir.

About Interactive Brokers Group, Inc.:
Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: IBKR) is a member of the S&P 500. Its affiliates provide automated trade execution and custody of securities, commodities, foreign exchange, and forecast contracts around the clock on over 170 markets in numerous countries and currencies from a single unified platform to clients worldwide. We serve individual investors, hedge funds, proprietary trading groups, financial advisors and introducing brokers. Our four decades of focus on technology and automation have enabled us to equip our clients with a uniquely sophisticated platform to manage their investment portfolios. We strive to provide our clients with advantageous execution prices and trading, risk and portfolio management tools, research facilities and investment products, all at low or no cost, positioning them to achieve superior returns on investments. Interactive Brokers has consistently earned recognition as a top broker, garnering multiple awards and accolades from respected industry sources such as Barron's, Investopedia, Stockbrokers.com, and many others.

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements:
The foregoing information contains certain forward-looking statements that reflect the company's current views with respect to certain current and future events and financial performance. These forward-looking statements are and will be, as the case may be, subject to many risks, uncertainties and factors relating to the company's operations and business environment which may cause the company's actual results to be materially different from any future results, expressed or implied, in these forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statements in this release are based upon information available to the company on the date of this release. The company does not undertake to publicly update or revise its forward-looking statements even if experience or future changes make it clear that any statements expressed or implied therein will not be realized. Additional information on risk factors that could potentially affect the company's financial results may be found in the company's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

US and World (except Europe): Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, X, YouTube, TikTok
UK and Europe: Facebook, Instagram, X, TikTok
